verification_steps: Recompute weighted_score_base for all records and assert equality with red_team_strata_plan.csv weighted_score column.
Compute weighted_score under +/-5% proportional perturbations and record numeric outputs.
Summarize budget_allocation.csv total and verify <= decision_budget_usd (205000).

planned_changes: Add sensitivity_checks.csv: for each record (R1..R4) include columns raw_* (scenario_severity, tool_access_level, historical_fail_rate, coverage_gap), norm_* (using declared divisors), weighted_score_base, weighted_score_minus5pct, weighted_score_plus5pct. Use proportional +/-5% perturbation on each raw metric, recompute norms and weighted_score, and include numeric values truncated to an appropriate precision (e.g., 6 decimals).

Add budget_allocation.csv: list proposed actions/interventions (paper_triage_R2, followup_review_R3, followup_review_R4, routine_monitor_R1), per-item incremental_usd (allow zeros), and total_incremental_usd. Mirror this table in eval_protocol.md.

Append a short 'validation' subsection to eval_protocol.md referencing sensitivity_checks.csv and budget_allocation.csv and include a one-line check that the weighted_score_base values match those in red_team_strata_plan.csv.

If eval_protocol.md preview truncation is a concern, also include sensitivity_checks.csv and budget_allocation.csv as separate deliverables (small CSVs) to ensure verifiers see the numeric tables in previews.

Run local verification to ensure recomputed weighted scores in the new sensitivity_checks.csv exactly match the baseline CSV for the base case and verify the +/-5% recomputations produce the stability statement claimed.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address minor documentation completeness issues: provide explicit per-record sensitivity numeric outputs and a minimal budget allocation table so verifiers can fully validate stability and budget statements.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are complete and reproducible: the CSV and protocol document the normalization divisors, weights, formulas, computed normalized metrics, weighted scores, ranking, and data-quality overrides. Deterministic and model graders verified calculations and safety constraints. Minor documentation completeness issues remain: the sensitivity-analysis section in eval_protocol.md was truncated in previews and does not include explicit per-record +/-5% perturbation numeric outputs, and the budget allocation is presented only as a single-line assertion ($0) instead of a small budget_allocation_table. Addressing these will make verification fully self-contained.
